events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: panelboard installation & makeup, conductors crossing bus gutter space. Work in progress. NEC Art. 408, 110.26, 200.4. Conductors crossing bus gutter space is a non-compliant condition - conductors should not cross the bus gutter space in a panelboard; they must be arranged neatly, not crossing over the bus. This is a common shortcut. NEC 408.3 requires conductors not cross the gutter space; 200.4 is about neutral conductors. Actually 200.4 is about neutral conductors being identified. But conductors crossing bus gutter space violates 408.3? Let me think.

NEC 408.3: Support and arrangement of busbars and conductors. It says conductors shall not be installed in the gutter space of a panelboard? Actually 408.3(A) says "Conductors shall not be installed in the gutter space of a panelboard." Wait, let me recall. NEC 408.3 covers "Support and Arrangement of Busbars and Conductors." 408.3(A) "Conductors shall not be installed in the gutter space of a panelboard." Hmm, actually I think it's 408.3(A) says conductors shall not be installed in the gutter space... no.

Let me recall: 408.3 Support and Arrangement of Busbars and Conductors. (A) Conductors shall not be installed in the gutter space of a panelboard. Actually I'm not 100% sure. But the scenario says "conductors crossing bus gutter space" which is a defect. This is non-compliant. Decision: fail. Risk: medium (could be high). Component conditions: non-compliant. Work stage: in-progress. Percent complete: maybe 60. Confidence high.

Let me write the JSON.

NEC 408 requires conductors to be arranged and supported so they do not occupy or cross the bus gutter improperly, and 110.26 requires clear working space around the panel. The work is in progress, so it must be corrected before proceeding.",
